title: Eleutheroglossum closterium
intro: Eleutheroglossum closterium, commonly known as the "clover-leaf fern", is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Eleutheroglossum closterium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other terpenoid compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is most commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in traditional herbal remedies such as tinctures and decoctions.
